summary: Carry each floor clause's node-scope predicate argument (bound/charset/keys/values) across the SDK→lock seam so the built-in lock losslessly encodes the floors, not just clause identity+severity

per.section: Decision: the built-in lock is derived from the SDK module, never transcribed

edit.description: `clauseRow()` (:78-100) today emits only the `count`/`membership`/`degree` argument for a requirement's set-scope clause and drops every node-scope predicate's own argument for a kind's `expect` (floor) clause — `min_len`/`max_len` bound (`predicate.args.min`/`.max`), `allowed_chars` charset, `forbidden_keys` keys, `deny` values, `max_lines` max. Copy each present argument onto the `ClauseRow`; extend the `ClauseRow` TS interface (:70-99) with the matching optional columns.

Update the `Predicate` doc comment (:18-24) that states `expect` clauses 'compile to a reduced row of key/field/severity only' — floor clauses now carry the full predicate payload. No constructor change: the args already ride `args`/`charset`/`keys`/`values` (:60-83).

`ClauseRow` (:974) gains the node-scope argument columns mirroring the SDK (a `{min,max}`-style bound for `min_len`/`max_len`/`max_lines`, a `Charset`-shaped column for `allowed_chars`, a key list for `forbidden_keys`, a value list for `deny`); `to_table`/`from_table` (:1266-1313) round-trip them alongside the existing `count`/`target`/`degree`. Admissibility's reject-unknown-keys stays — the new columns are known.

`effective` (:106) matches a committed-lock override by predicate key + targeted field and flips severity in place — unchanged, but the ClauseRow it reads now carries args, so verify no assumption that a floor ClauseRow is argument-less. The compiled-Clause→ClauseRow construction the lock emit uses populates the new columns.

Regenerate via the memberless emit (`builtin_lock.rs` header ritual) so every floor `[[declaration.clause]]` row carries its node-scope argument (skill `name` max_len=64, min_len=1, allowed_chars charset, deny values, forbidden_keys keys, max_lines=500; rule/memory likewise). Fix the stale header comment: 3 kind rows not 'four built-in kinds' (:3), and drop the dangling `BUILTIN-LOCK-ROW-DRIVEN` tag reference (:22) — rust.md exit clause.

tests.asserts: a floor clause row round-trips its node-scope argument — the skill `name` max_len row carries max=64, the forbidden_keys row carries its key list, the allowed_chars row carries its charset — through emit→lock→read_declarations
the re-derived built-in lock still byte-matches the embedded `builtin_lock.toml` after the argument columns land (the derivation and the embed move together)
clauseRow() serializes a node-scope predicate's argument (a max_len bound, a forbidden_keys list) onto the emitted ClauseRow

acceptance: The lock's floor `[[declaration.clause]]` rows carry the full predicate payload (identity·argument·severity·field·guidance·cite), so a floor Contract is reconstructable from the rows alone; builtin_lock.toml regenerated and byte-frozen; SDK + Rust round-trip tests green.
notes: New foundation link — the LOCK-CLAUSE-CHANNELS (eca35e6) analogue for predicate arguments. Today `expect` clauses compile a deliberately reduced row (contract.ts:18) that only needed identity+severity because builtin.rs carried the args; BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION removes that hand-written home, so the lock must encode the floors losslessly first. Unblocks the projection.

summary: Reimplement builtin.rs's floor data as a projection of the built-in lock's clause rows (full predicate payload carried) — no hand-written clause vectors; retire the package noun

per.section: Decision: the built-in lock is derived from the SDK module, never transcribed

edit.description: contract()/contracts() build each kind's floor Contract from the parsed built-in lock's ClauseRows (predicate·argument·field·severity·guidance·cite) instead of the hand-written skill_anthropic()/rule_anthropic()/memory_anthropic() clause vectors and the *_GUIDANCE consts; drop SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE/MEMORY_ANTHROPIC_PACKAGE and BUILTIN_PACKAGE_NAMES. Floors keyed by kind row label. Retire the `package std-lib`/PACKAGE.md doc comments and the `packages/<name>/PACKAGE.md` curated-tree citations (rust.md exit clause).

Any residual package-name floor lookup in the check dispatch (BUILTIN_FLOOR_BINDINGS :65-73, builtin_floor/floor_package :575-586) becomes a kind-label lookup over the projection (most already flattened by BUILTIN-KIND-FLATTEN).
The `explain` floor-name rendering (bound_package :156-157, :398-400) names the floor by its kind label, not a `<kind>.<source>` package name.

tests.asserts: the rule floor is the lock-projected contract keyed by the `rule` kind; its display name is the kind label, not `rule.anthropic`; declared severities, arguments, and guidance survive the projection
the governing-floor line names the floor by its kind label (the `.anthropic` package names are gone); why_collaboration/why_lint_runner snapshots update alike

acceptance: builtin.rs carries no hand-written clause or guidance data — every built-in floor is a projection of the embedded lock's clause rows (predicate/argument/severity/field/guidance/cite); the package noun and its `<kind>.<source>` names are gone; contract_template.rs/acceptance.rs floor-name assertions update; gates green.
notes: Second consumer link. Blocked now on LOCK-CLAUSE-PREDICATE-ARGS (the floor rows drop the node-scope argument today — contract.ts:18 'reduced row' — so the projection needs the full predicate first). BUILTIN-KIND-FLATTEN shipped (3712d24). Contract-the-Rust-type stays (code owns the type name; rename out of scope). Closes the derived-lock Decision's `no hand-written mirror of the SDK's floors`.

summary: check models only the built-in kinds — read the committed lock's custom kind rows so custom-kind requirements resolve instead of false admissibility failures (cascade field report)

per.section: The lock and drift — one vocabulary

edit.description: The gate builds its kind set from the built-in default program only and reads the committed lock (`drift::read_declarations`) for clauses/requirements/satisfies but NOT for kinds — so an adopted harness's `declarations.kinds` (its custom kind rows, written whole by emit — cascade saw 9 spec kinds + 74 members in a fresh lock) are invisible, and a requirement naming a custom kind fails `requirement.admissibility` ("names kind X, which temper does not model") with an `unfilled` shadow. Feed the committed lock's `KindFactRow`s into the check kind loop, merged over the built-in default program, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind.

`Workspace::load_kinds` / the members-map keying must load members for the committed lock's custom kind rows (their loci/layout), not only the built-in kind set, so a custom kind's members are conformance-checked (cascade: `check --harness .` checked only the built-ins).

Kind-blind extraction over the committed lock's custom kind facts (locus, layout, registration) — the same generic path the built-in kinds ride, sourced from the lock rows. Reassess whether any engine.rs edit is needed once the chain lands: extraction may already be fully generic and the fix confined to check.rs/main.rs feeding it the rows.

tests.asserts: a harness whose committed lock declares a custom kind and a requirement naming it does NOT emit a false requirement.admissibility failure; the requirement resolves against the lock-declared kind and reports genuine coverage only

acceptance: `temper check` (and its session-start reporter) reads custom kind declaration rows from the committed lock, so a custom-kind requirement resolves against its declared kind instead of failing admissibility as unmodeled; the session-start gate no longer cries wolf on any harness with a custom-kind requirement.
notes: Cascade field report, 07-06 — the false blockers made cascade disable its SessionStart hook (law-3 'gate that cries wolf gets disabled'). Serialized behind the derived-lock chain: it edits the same check/main kind-resolution code the chain reworks. When the chain ships, plan re-checks whether the row-driven path already reads the committed lock's custom kinds — if so, drop this.

summary: Whole-tree comment sweep — prose spec recaps to pointer tags, delete era + compliance narration and retired-tree citations per rust.md's taxonomy; no behavior change

gate.reason: Whole-tree sweep — must run solo (a broad src/+tests/ comment edit races any concurrent entry). Ride after the derived-lock chain lands: the chain rewrites the two heaviest-commented files (builtin.rs/builtin_kind.rs) on contact, so sweeping them first is throwaway. Plan promotes this to a solo open entry once the chain ships and the queue is otherwise empty.

edit.description: Representative anchor for the residue the chain leaves untouched. Per rust.md's taxonomy + exit clause: drop the `kinds/<name>/KIND.md` curated-tree citations (//! header + :26 — the tree is slated for physical deletion, a human chore), convert prose spec recaps to terse pointer tags (file path only), delete era narration ('no longer X', 'any more'), compliance narration, and dangling pending-tag references. Module //! headers to ~10 lines.

Representative anchor for the same sweep across the crate's other over-commented modules and their tests — e.g. the `packages/` curated-tree reference (:79) and main.rs's 'Custom kinds retire with the KIND.md file format' comments (:401/:424/:632/:894/:924).

acceptance: Zero quoted spec section titles in src/ or tests/ comments; zero era vocabulary from 90-spec-system's migration trail; no comment referencing a pending tag that isn't in the queue; no citation of the retired `kinds/`/`packages/` curated trees; module //! headers ~10 lines; cargo gates green — no behavior change.
notes: Ceremony item (John 07-06). rust.md's exit clause cuts comments on contact for files the chain touches (builtin.rs by the projection); this sweep is the residue in the files the chain leaves untouched (builtin_kind.rs/bundle.rs/main.rs KIND.md refs), run once at the end. Deferred (not blockedBy) because a whole-tree edit races every concurrent entry — promoted to a solo open entry after the chain fully lands and the queue is otherwise empty.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
